regimeSub: Risk-on reflation as Iran ceasefire caps oil, but 4.2% CPI and a hawkish Warsh Fed kill the cutting bias and tee up hikes.
themes: Iran-US deal halts attacks -> oil rerates lower, equities melt up on energy relief
CPI at 4.2% 3yr high + Warsh removes cut bias -> September HIKE now live, not cuts
AI/energy capex supercycle -> tech leadership drives Dow >52k, SPX 7440 on narrow breadth
Trump-Fed clash (Cook firing blocked) -> credibility risk underpins gold at $3,990 despite firm dollar

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = mildly negative real; still loose given inflation reaccel. Warsh wants tighter.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp (4.07/4.38), modestly steep; bear-steepening risk if Fed hikes into sticky inflation.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17.65 and equity highs signal no credit stress, complacency on hike risk.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65 — calm. Iran de-escalation crushed the geopolitical premium; underpricing Fed hawkish turn.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73 after US-Iran deal; overshoot to downside per ING. Supply-surge pricing not guaranteed — upside skew.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $3,992 near records — hedging Fed independence fears (Cook/Trump) and sticky inflation, not war.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1548 weak won despite KOSPI 8615 boom; carry and JPY weakness (162) pressuring Asia FX.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/energy-trade leadership powering chips; the $7T AI boom is the dominant risk-on engine.

headline: Markets are ripping to record highs as a US-Iran ceasefire caps oil, but a 3-year-high 4.2% CPI and Warsh's hawkish Fed have flipped the conversation from cuts to a September hike.
